author: Arlekin64
title: Trop lent ! Et mensonger…
content:
Beaucoup TROP lent dans la récupération des albums et morceaux de musique qui sont sur le disque dur de l’iMac. Les morceaux apparaissent petit à petit, lentement, très lentement, mais alors vraiment très lentement, c’est incroyable.
iTunes récupère ma musique en 2 minutes, Flacbox… je ne sais pas, j’ai abandonné au bout de 1h30 d’attente…

De plus, quand on s’abonne, ou qu’on achète l’application, il est bien indiqué que l’on peut utiliser l’application sur tous les appareils connectés au compte iTunes. Ce qui est faux, il faut repasser à la caisse pour l’iPhone/iPad… :(

Dommage car l’esthétique et les fonctionnalités me plaisaient bien et je l’aurai bien utilisée s’il n’y avait pas ces désagréments…

content: This app is almost worthless to me. I followed the developer’s guide to connecting to my external drive.  It seemed like it was adding files and folders, took quite a while.  After it’s finished reading and synching the files on the drive…….nothing. It shows no music was added at all. I have similar apps that do the same thing as this, but ACTUALLY WORK, and let me play music. This app needs to go back to the drawing board, and talk to someone who knows how to do this ‘music file syncing’ thing. Until then, my advice is….try a different app.

title: Guter Player mit Schwächen
content: Der Import von Offline-Dateien, vor allem wenn es viele sind, ist schwierig, weil man die Alben einzeln synchronisieren muss. Der Player hat kein Gapless-Play. Der Support ist dahingehend feedbackresistent (und träge), man wird vertröstet (hat keine große Priorität etc), dabei sollte Gapless Play eigentlich Standard sein. Schade, wenn man für diesen Player schon bezahlt hat. Klingt gut, die App und kann HiRes, aber für meine Bedürfnisse nicht befriedigend, ich suche aktuell nach einer guten und nicht so überfrachteten Alternative.

author: Strawbs03
title: Fazit zur FlacBox
content: ​FlacBox ist ein vielseitiger Hi-Res-Musikplayer für iOS und macOS, der sich besonders für Nutzer eignet, die ihre eigene verlustfreie Musiksammlung verwalten und abspielen möchten. FlacBox ist eine leistungsstarke App für Audiophile und Nutzer mit umfangreichen Musiksammlungen, die Wert auf verlustfreie Wiedergabe und flexible Speicheroptionen legen. Wünschenswert wären Druckfunktionen wie Titel aus Playlisten, sowohl das Anlegen von Unterordner. Auch der Import von Playlisten mit mehreren Ebenen wird ignoriert, es werden ausschließlich die Titel importiert. Ansonsten eine gute Anwendung mit vielen positiven Eigenschaften.

author: Aidyc1
title: Good once your music library has synced.
content: Took a full week to finish syncing meta data from my nas drive, even at full speed. Would be great to have the audio player button in the sidebar at the top. Would also like to see a seperate 'compilations' section. other than that, this is the best music player of its kind i have come across.

author: Whitefish Heineken 1849
title: Beautiful AND functional!
content:
Flacbox delivers exactly what music lovers need—a polished app that prioritizes listening without sacrificing power. Here's why it's become my daily driver:

Lightning-fast performance with instant library scans and gapless playback
Frequent updates that consistently add value (6 major upgrades in 2024 alone)
Audiophile-grade tools like parametric EQ and bass boost without UI clutter
Universal format support – plays FLAC/DSD/APE files natively
Smart subscription model - only $15 a year
The minimalist interface keeps focus on album art and playback controls, while advanced features stay accessible through thoughtful gestures. Cloud integration (11+ services supported) makes my cloud library feel local.

For anyone tired of bloated music apps, Flacbox proves simplicity and sophistication can coexist – all at a price that puts premium DAC companies to shame. Essential for lossless audio enthusiasts!

author: Nick996677
title: Buy it once, use it every day
content:
Musical obsessive, digital packrat, staying up late nights to download, going wild on Bandcamp Friday, any of those sound familar? Apple’s decision to not support FLAC but instead introduce their own parallel scheme, ALAC, that requires 4x the CPU power that FLAC requires. Typical heavy-handed “you’ll take what we give you and like it” Apple approach.

So, I bought Flacbox for life, and was happy using it on my iPhone but now that it’s available on macOS, it’s even more useful. Music libraries that are split across directories or even volumes (NFS shares, etc.) are easy to integrate. Some of the power features are not totally obvious so some time with the documentation is useful.

author: badprogger
title: Flacbox is a game-changer for audiophiles!
content:
As someone who takes audio quality seriously, Flacbox is hands-down the best app I’ve found for playing my lossless music files. I’ve been using it on my Mac, and it has been a fantastic experience so far.

Key features that stand out to me:

- Supports all my audio formats: I primarily use FLAC, but it handles a ton of formats, including ALAC, MP3, and even DSD. No more worrying about converting files!

- Built-in equalizer and preamp: The customizable equalizer really lets me tweak the sound to exactly how I want it. I love that it also has a preamp to boost sound without distortion.

- Cloud support: I can connect my Google Drive, Dropbox, and even OneDrive accounts to stream my music directly, which is a lifesaver since I keep a large collection in the cloud.

- Bookmarking and playlist management: I can easily bookmark specific tracks and organize everything into playlists. It makes managing a large library so much easier.

Honestly, the app is smooth, responsive, and looks great on macOS. I’ve seen regular updates too, so it’s clear the developers are listening to feedback and improving the app. Highly recommended for anyone who loves high-quality audio!

title: macos mu uygulama mı?
content: macos mu kötü uygulama mı? Bence al birini vur ötekine. Apple aşıkları koyunluklarına devam etsinler. Cihazlar çok kaliteli olabilir ama kullanışlılık, esneklik, kişiselleştirilebilirlik sıfır. Apple nasıl istiyorsa öyle. Doğru dürüst bir müzik uygulaması bulamadım. En kötü Windows müzik uygulaması en iyi Mac müzik uygulamasından 10 kat daha kullanışlı. Apple Music bile, bırakın diğer platformları macos'ta bile iğrenç çalışıyor. Windows bilgisayarlar varken neden macos kullanayım. Bir Mac'im olsun diye mi? Çok komik.

author: Elon Tim
title: From Sky to dustbean.
content:
This has turned into a complete disappointment and is now a mess. I am taking down my social media video where I praised it as the best music app.

Initially, I considered this app one of the best and even created a social media video to recommend it. However, it seems they've ruined it now, and I've decided to remove my video.

It used to be an excellent app, but now it's not worth using. Instead of asking for $14, the developers should consider giving refunds. The user interface is confusing, copied files don't display properly, and it's all quite chaotic. In my opinion, they should either improve it, return to its previous state, or consider other options altogether. I apologize for my strong language, but this app is incredibly disappointing.

Moreover, it's puzzling that the app is available for both Mac and iOS, yet it can't sync files on the same network. They seem unable to fix these simple issues. The sound quality has deteriorated significantly, and no matter how I adjust the EQ settings, it doesn't sound as it used to. The sound now has too much bass and is distorted, and the beats that I once enjoyed are no longer clear. Instead, I hear noise rather than music.

In summary, even if it's now available for free, it's not worth it. I recommend considering alternatives like Foobar or VLC, which, despite their less-than-perfect user interfaces, are not as bad as this app has become.

author: meekmillan
title: Useless if you use an external drive
content:
TLDR; You can’t browse folders and sub folders from external drives in the app to play music without it being in one BIG playlist.

Currently you have to drag and drop or go to “Open Folders” and either open the specifc folder (album for example) you want to play or select the folder that it exists in (artist folder or all of your music library for example). From there it opens up everything inside that folder and puts it in one BIG playlist, regardless if any of the songs came from subfolders.
Thats not the worst if its just one album but for people that keep their music library on an external drive its a total deal breaker.
Not to mention music often has unfilled/mixed meta data like multiple artists being apart of 1 song on an album which makes sorting innefective.
There NEEDS to be a way to browse folders the same way you would in Mac finder/Windows explorer. Because of this I always have to drag and drop individual folders (albums) into the player and go from there. Its super frustrating.

And a related issue when opening files from the "Files on this MAC" tab or from an external drive, you can’t automatically sort/rearrange songs by title/file name/artist/etc etc. You’re stuck with the order that FLAC Box throws at you unless you want to individually rearrange each song. Again.. not THAT big of a deal when it’s one album but when its hundreds or thousands of songs it means this app isn’t an option.

author: Sheryll234
title: Severe bug exist for months!
content:
The player control crash was fixed after several months, but it is now crashing again when opening external folders in the latest version update!

A new bug has been added!!

I believe the developers have not abandoned this app, but it is clear that they are no longer actively maintaining it. Updates are few and far between, and even serious bugs are not fixed immediately!

author: Paul Mina
title: Does not correct volume for replay gain
content: I have a number of recordings that all play back at different volumes. All my audio has replay gain tags so in other audio players everthing plays at the right volume. I have to give you one star as it is very dangerous when I'm driving and all of a sudden the volume is on full blast giving me a fright in traffic. This is unacceptable!!! Once you fix this I will update this review, untill then I need to find something else.

author: emily.st
title: Incredible compatibility, vaguely bewildering UI, some missing features
content:
I got this app so that I can play media off of a Plex server using DLNA. This works surprising well, and it's the only app of its kind that seems to allow what I want. It generally does this well, and it might go the distance with me.

It features a fully flexible sleep timer, fine-grained control of media/metadata synchronization, AirPlay/Bluetooth, fine-grained queue support, and a number of other little features I really wanted.

There are several rough spots, however, so I'm not giving this a perfect rating. Listing below:

What is the difference between this and Evermusic? I literally cannot tell.

Libraries/files/metadata cannot be stored nor synced in iCloud Drive, despite being a native macOS/iOS app.

DLNA synchronization is painfully slow. It is also deeply confusing because Plex exposes its library several times over in different ways in DLNA, along with its *preferences*?? How weird.

Having online music, offline music, and external files in different places in the app is really confusing. It would seem preferable to expose, instead, simply a library, whose underlying files could be located locally, externally, or online.

Reminders of how to use the app interface are intrusive and cannot be disabled.

Settings are laid out in an odd way. "User interface" stuff probably belongs grouped in with the settings for that part of the interface it actually affects. 

Deeply frustrating that there are very poor options for sorting and viewing. By default, for example, genres are presented as a *list of songs*. Under artists, you can choose to view songs or albums, which is inconsistent with genre (which does not give you this choice). The only way to see, say, artists or albums together under a genre is to sort by one or the other to group those songs together. There's no concept of sorting by multiple things (like artist then year).

Play queues can be edited, but certain nice things are missing, like the ability to clear everything in the queue *after* the song you're playing. There is also no easy, one-click way to play a single track without playing all the others (best way available is to choose "Play Next" and then start playback, a two-step process).

Airplay works, but it gets easily confused about how to set the volume for an Airplay device.

I'll be watching to see how this app develops in the future. Thanks!
